title: 'Why and where are you going to transmute' — Sept 2020 title-only seed
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only Apple Note from Sept 5, 2020, 11:05pm — Yahir is 17, senior year at Grants Pass High during the COVID pandemic remote-school chapter. Title is the entire artifact: "Why and where are you going to transmute." Body is empty. 11-second create→modify gap.
  • Same-day companion to the "Coulter questions" note (3:58am earlier) — Sept 5, 2020 is a single-day double-note spanning logistics (counselor questions) at dawn and metaphysical self-interrogation at night. The whole arc of register lives in one date.
  • Pure manifesto style note essay-prompt-never-written in the terminal-form. Title is a question Yahir asks himself; body is the silence where the answer would have been.
  • "Transmute" is alchemical / occult language — the verb shows up in self-help-adjacent traditions (Hill's Think and Grow Rich "sex transmutation," Jungian shadow-work). Yahir at 17 is reading or absorbing material that uses this vocabulary; the title is a personal-application question ("apply transmutation here, to what?") rather than a definitional inquiry.
  • Sits in the 2020 senior-year metaphysics cluster alongside the same-day Coulter counselor questions note and Yahir's broader pandemic-era reading. Anticipates the late-2021 "Setting your heart ablaze" manifesto-form and the love as sacrifice aphorism.

Source

"Why and where are you going to transmute"

Why it matters in the graph

  • The earliest explicit-self-address-in-second-person note in the corpus — Yahir asking himself a question he intends to answer later. The form predicts the entire note as letter to self genre.
  • Same-date pairing with the Coulter logistics note demonstrates the high-low register switching that defines as a form.

Open questions

  • Source for the transmute vocabulary? Hill, Jung, a YouTube channel, a specific book?
  • Did Yahir ever write the answer? No follow-up note in the corpus.
